results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE OBJECTIVE OF THE PROJECT IS TO INVESTIGATE ENHANCING INDOOR AIR QUALITY BY RESEARCHING ALTERNATIVE RADON MITIGATION SYSTEMS FOR BUILDING OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE. THE STUDY AIMS AT FINDING THE SIGNIFICANT IMPACT OF BUILDING OCCUPANT'S DAILY PRACTICES ON LEVEL OF INDOOR RADON IN KENTUCKY.ACTIVITIES:THE RESEARCHERS WILL EXAMINE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF USING THREE BUILDING DESIGN PARAMETERS THAT HAVEN'T BEEN SYSTEMATICALLY USED FOR RADON MITIGATION IN THE PAST; VENTILATION, AIR FILTRATION, AND DETAILING PATTERN. THE STUDY WILL UTILIZE 9 DIGITAL MONITORS THAT WILL BE INSTALLED IN THE 6 HOUSES TO LOG DATA ABOUT INDOOR RADON LEVEL AROUND THE CLOCK FOR THE PERIOD OF THE STUDY. THE DATA INCLUDES RADON PCI/L, TEMPERATURE DEG;F, HUMIDITY %RH, AND PRESSURE KPA. THE PURPOSE IS TO MONITOR THE LEVEL OF RADON IN THESE HOUSES THROUGHOUT THE COLD MONTHS IN BOWLING GREEN, THE SEASON THAT HAS THE HIGHEST LEVELS OF NATURAL RADON GAS.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLE OF THIS EXPERIMENT INCLUDE NEW MEASURABLE GUIDELINE THAT CAN MITIGATE THE LEVEL OF RADON CONCENTRATION IN HOUSES USING REGULAR MAINTENANCE PRACTICES. THE GRANTEES WITH ALSO PROVIDE A REPORT AT THE END OF THE PROJECT SHARING THEIR RESULTS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE IMPROVED INDOOR AIR QUALITY AND IMPROVED HUMAN HEALTH. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT WILL BE INDIVIDUALS AND COMMUNITIES IN KENTUCKY AND ACROSS THE U.S., PARTICULARLY PEOPLE IN LOW-INCOME AREAS AFFECTED BY HIGH RADON LEVELS.
